title: BCBS 239 - Principles for effective risk data aggregation and risk reporting
summary: Basel Committee standard establishing principles for risk data aggregation and reporting capabilities. Requires banks to have strong data governance, architecture, and IT infrastructure to support effective risk management.

key_requirements.title: Principle 1: Governance
key_requirements.description: Board and senior management must ensure strong governance and infrastructure for risk data aggregation and reporting

key_requirements.title: Principle 2: Data architecture and IT infrastructure
key_requirements.description: Bank must design, build and maintain data architecture and IT infrastructure to support risk data aggregation

key_requirements.title: Principle 3: Accuracy and Integrity
key_requirements.description: Risk data must be accurate and reliable with reconciliation processes and validation controls

key_requirements.title: Principle 4: Completeness
key_requirements.description: Risk data must capture all material risk exposures and positions

key_requirements.title: Principle 5: Timeliness
key_requirements.description: Risk data must be available in time to support risk management decisions

key_requirements.title: Principle 6: Adaptability
key_requirements.description: Risk data aggregation capabilities must be flexible to meet ad-hoc requests

engineering_implications.description: Implement unified data model with clear lineage and reconciliation points
Build automated data quality checks, validation rules, and reconciliation processes
Maintain complete audit trail of data transformations and aggregations
Design scalable, flexible architecture supporting ad-hoc queries and stress scenarios

source_references.citation: BCBS 239: Principles for effective risk data aggregation and risk reporting
